Method.desc: Configures the spoon. Accepts any of the public variables (delay, excludedApps).
Method.doc:
Configures the spoon. Accepts any of the public variables (delay, excludedApps).

Parameters:
 * configuration - a table of configuration values to merge into the spoon
Method.name: configure
parameters: * configuration - a table of configuration values to merge into the spoon

Method.desc: Returns the topmost standard window whose frame contains the given point, or nil.
Method.doc:
Returns the topmost standard window whose frame contains the given point, or nil.

Parameters:
 * point - a table with x and y fields (e.g. from hs.mouse.absolutePosition())
Method.name: windowUnderPoint
parameters: * point - a table with x and y fields (e.g. from hs.mouse.absolutePosition())

Method.desc: Starts focusing windows as the mouse moves over them.
Method.doc:
Starts focusing windows as the mouse moves over them.

Parameters:
 * None
Method.name: start

Method.desc: Stops focusing windows as the mouse moves.
Method.doc:
Stops focusing windows as the mouse moves.

Parameters:
 * None
Method.name: stop

Variable.desc: Seconds the pointer must rest over a window before focus is changed. Defaults to 0.1.
Variable.doc: Seconds the pointer must rest over a window before focus is changed. Defaults to 0.1.
Variable.name: delay

Variable.desc: List of app names or bundle IDs whose windows should never be auto-focused.
Variable.doc: List of app names or bundle IDs whose windows should never be auto-focused.
Variable.name: excludedApps

Variable.desc: Logger object used within the Spoon. Can be accessed to set the default log level for the messages coming from the Spoon.
Variable.doc: Logger object used within the Spoon. Can be accessed to set the default log level for the messages coming from the Spoon.
Variable.name: logger

desc: Focus the window under the mouse pointer ("sloppy focus" / X11-style focus-follows-mouse).
doc:
Focus the window under the mouse pointer ("sloppy focus" / X11-style focus-follows-mouse). When the pointer comes to rest over a window that isn't already focused, that window is focused.

A short debounce delay means focus only changes once the cursor settles, so quickly sweeping the pointer across windows does not thrash focus. Focus changes are suppressed while a mouse button is held, so dragging never steals focus.

Note: on macOS, focusing a window also raises it; there is no system primitive for focus-without-raise.
name: FocusFollowsMouse
